They’re baffled by the metric system and write their dates backward.

Now, the US has found another way to skew the world order by reversing itsOlympicmedal count to value total medals over gold.

Apparently, they’ve always done it that way.

At the time of writing on Monday, Japan leads the medal tally with four gold and a total of seven medals.

Australia follows closely behind with an equal four gold medals and six total medals.

Trailing behind, powerhouse USA has claimed just three gold medals after two days of official competition, yet boasts the highest cumulative medal count with 12.

According to a host of American publications, the home of more than 330 million people is entitled to claim it’s “leading” the medal count due to having the highest total number of medals.

Yet that’s not how the official Paris Olympics leaderboard sees it as the official leaderboard counts gold medals rather than total medals.

US news channel NBC is one example of an outlet purporting a clear early dominance from those donning the star-spangled banner.

AndXusers from around the world are not happy about it.

“NBC always does the medal count wrong to make the US look better and it’s so shameless. It’s ranked by number of gold medals, not total medals you idiots,” one person posted.

“No, it’s not in the US. I’ve been watching since 1972 and it’s the way we’ve always done it,” another responded.

Bleacher ReportandESPNare two other American publications that appear to value “Total Medals By Country” over gold medals won.

One X user noted even The New York Times has chosen to focus on total medal count, posting a tally after Day 1 of the competition which had the USA second place overall behind Australia, despite claiming only one gold medal to that point.

Keen Aussie followers of the Games would know Australia typically fades in the second week of competition where pool events are traded for track and field.

The historic medal count is well in favour of the Americans, with 1061 gold medals out of their total 2635 up to 2020’s Games, according to Statista.

Australia comes in around twelfth on the list with 167 gold from 553 medals.

Olympic Games

‘Worst one yet’
title: Paris Olympics 2024: American media outlets slammed for reporting ‘wrong’ rankings | PerthNow
description: They’re baffled by the metric system and write their dates backward — now the US has found another way to skew the world order by reversing their Olympic medal count to value total medals over gold.
